beats.narration: In immersion testing the probe never touches the part. A column of water carries the sound — and water is a consistent couplant.

payload.title: IMMERSION TESTING
payload.sub: The transducer never touches the part.

beats.narration: Three ways to get water in place: dunk the part in a tank, flow a squirter column, or roll a liquid filled wheel.

payload.heading: Three ways to put water in the path

beats.narration: Sound crosses water before metal, so the first big echo is the part's front surface. Everything inside the metal appears to its right.

payload.heading: Reading the immersion A-scan
payload.note: METAL ECHOES LAND RIGHT OF THE FRONT SURFACE

beats.narration: The water has rules: keep it clean and bubble free, keep squirter columns laminar, and set the entry surface square to the beam.

chips.t: Entry surface square to the beam

beats.narration: So remember: in immersion, water is the couplant. Keep it clean, bubble free, and steady, and the setup repeats exactly, part after part.

payload.sub: Keep it clean, bubble-free, and steady.
